I have a complete picture. Here is the concise map of the TUI state machine relevant to your code-switching mode.

## 1. Keybindings, toggles & mode state

Bindings are declared in `internal/tui/keys.go:7-45` (struct + `defaultKeys`):
- `^g` `TogglePair` (`keys.go:34`), `^n` `ToggleLearn` (`keys.go:35`), `^y` `Copy` (`keys.go:36`)
- Pickers: `^e` `CycleEngine`, `^t` `PickLang`, `^p` `PickModel`, `^o` `PickPreset` (`keys.go:30-33`); plus `^l` live, `^s` speak, `^r` history, `^u` clear, `⇥` focus.

Handlers live in `update.go handleKey` (`update.go:166-323`), a `switch key.Matches(...)` block. Toggle pattern (identical for `^g`/`^n`):
- `ToggleLearn` `update.go:211-233`: guards on `p.LearnEngine == nil` → flash; flips `m.learn`; forces `m.pair = true` and picks a distinct `pairWith`; `m.relayout()`; then if `live && non-empty` re-launches, else `m.clearResult()`.
- `TogglePair` `update.go:235-250`: flips `m.pair`, picks distinct `pairWith`, relayout, live-relaunch-or-clearResult.

Mode state on the Model (`model.go:96-102`): `source`, `target`, `pair bool`, `pairWith string`, `learn bool`, `preset string`, `live bool`, `engIdx int` (active engine via `active()` `model.go:148-153`). Engine mode also comes from `active().Mode` (`engine.ModeDict`/`ModeTranslate`). Picker/overlay state: `overlay overlayKind` (`model.go:91`); overlay kinds enumerated in `pickers.go:14-24` (`overlayNone/History/Lang/Model/Suggest/Preset`).

Key routing note: an open overlay captures all keys first (`update.go:167-181`). Learn mode intercepts `^e`/`^p`/`^o` as no-ops/flash (`update.go:198-209`, `271-274`) because it owns its own prompt.

Initial mode wiring: `Params` (`model.go:29-47`) carries `Pair`, `Learn`, `LearnEngine`, etc.; `New` (`model.go:202-225`) sets `learn: p.Learn && p.LearnEngine != nil`.

## 2. Result rendering

Dispatch is `renderResult` (`view.go:188-219`): switches on `res.Learn != nil` → `renderLearn`; `res.Dictionary != nil` → `renderDictionary`; `len(res.Suggestions)>0` → `renderSuggestions`; else plain. Plain path renders `st.trans.Render(res.Translation)` then appends detected-source, `~` alternatives, `ⓘ` notes, `⚠` warnings (`view.go:198-218`).

Learn rendering `renderLearn` (`view.go:243-300`) — this is the reusable pattern you want. It branches on `l.Direction`:
- `"correct"`: `✔ <corrected>` as the headline (falls back to `res.Translation`), dim native translation, then per-`Issue` `✎ span → fix` + indented explanation (`view.go:246-266`).
- `"teach"` (else): headline = `l.Translation` (foreign sentence), then per-`Vocab` gloss `term (pos) phonetic` + native meaning, then `Examples` (`✎ foreign` / `↳ native`) (`view.go:267-292`).
- Common tail: `ⓘ notes` + `⚠ warnings`.

Key structural fact: the plain foreign sentence is always the first line (`st.trans.Render(...)`), and the structured extras (vocab/issues/examples/notes) are appended after. This is exactly the "one translation line + glosses below" shape your code-switching mode needs.

Footer/status: `statusLine` → `footerContent(false)` (`view.go:88, 103-184`). It has three mode-specific branches, each ending in its own dim help string:
- Learn branch `view.go:125-143`: label `learn zh⇄en`, no engine cycle/style, help ends `↵ learn  ^y copy … ^n exit …`.
- Dictionary branch `view.go:146-155`.
- Default translate branch `view.go:157-183`: label `pair zh⇄en` or `src→tgt`, `engineSeg`, `style:<preset>`, `live●/○`, help `↵ translate  ^y copy … ^g pair …`.

`footerHeight` (`view.go:93-98`) reserves the widest state for stable layout; a new mode adds rows here if its footer wraps.

## 3. Copy behavior (^y)

Handler `update.go:252-261` calls `m.copyText()` and writes it via `clipboard.WriteAll`. `copyText` (`update.go:498-503`):
```go
if m.result == nil { return "" }
return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
```
So today it already copies only `res.Translation` — the plain string, NOT the formatted block — for both plain and learn results. Because learn mode stores the main foreign sentence in `Translation` too (corrected sentence or translation; see `engine.go:81-84` and `llm.go:318-320`), `^y` already copies just the sentence in every mode. For your code-switching mode you'd populate `res.Translation` with the completed foreign sentence and `^y` "copy only the translation" works with zero changes. (Speak `update.go:517-526` uses the same `res.Translation`, falling back to `Dictionary.Word`.)

## 4. Result invalidation

`clearResult` (`update.go:488-495`) is the shared invalidator: cancels in-flight, nils `result`, clears `streamBuf`/`err`, sets `statusIdle`, blanks the viewport. The universal pattern after any setting change is: mutate state → `relayout()` → `if m.live && non-empty { return m.launch(...) } else { m.clearResult() }`. Call sites:
- `^n` learn `update.go:229-232`; `^g` pair `update.go:246-249`; `^e` engine `update.go:281-285` (also resets `curEngine/curModel`).
- In `overlaySelect`: lang `update.go:618-621`, model `update.go:628-631`, preset `update.go:647-650`.

Separately, live typing on empty input clears the result inline (`update.go:345-354`), and `launch` guards against re-running an identical result via `lastDoneKey`/`cache` (`update.go:386-410`).

## State-machine map for your code-switching mode

Adding a new toggle alongside `^g`/`^n`:
1. Add a `key.Binding` field + `defaultKeys` entry in `keys.go` (pick a free `ctrl+*`).
2. Add a `codeSwitch bool` (or an enum if modes are mutually exclusive with learn — note learn currently forces `pair=true`) field to `Model` (`model.go:96-102`) + init in `New` (`model.go:202-225`) + `Params` if it should start on.
3. Add a `case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleX)` in `handleKey` (`update.go:183-323`) mirroring `ToggleLearn` (`update.go:211-233`): guard on engine availability, flip state, set pair direction, `relayout()`, live-relaunch-or-`clearResult()`.
4. In `launch` (`update.go:380-473`), branch like the learn block (`update.go:452-462`): swap to the LLM engine, set a new request flag (`req.Learn`-style), force non-streaming, set pair home/away. This requires an engine-layer flag (see below).

Rendering translation + vocab glosses:
- Reuse the `LearnResult` shape (`engine.go:122-152`): `Translation` = the completed foreign sentence, `Vocab []LearnGloss` = glosses of the filled-in words (`Term/Pos/Phonetic/Meaning`), `Issues []LearnIssue` = grammar corrections (`Span/Fix/Explanation`). Your mode maps almost 1:1 onto the existing "teach" + "correct" fields — you may not need a new result type at all, just a new `Direction` value or a new `req` flag that produces this payload.
- Add a `renderCodeSwitch` (or extend `renderLearn` `view.go:243-300`) and dispatch in `renderResult` (`view.go:188-197`). The `trans`-headline + appended `notes`/`alt`/`dim` styling is directly reusable (styles in `internal/tui/styles.go`).
- Add a footer branch in `footerContent` (`view.go:103-184`) with its own help string; update `footerHeight` reservation if needed.

Copying only the translation: already handled — populate `res.Translation` with the completed sentence and `copyText` (`update.go:498-503`) does the right thing. No change needed unless you want `^y` to optionally copy the glosses too.

Reusable patterns to note: (a) the `Translation`-holds-the-plain-sentence / structured-extras-ride-alongside convention (`engine.go:81-84`) is exactly the separation you need — copy/history/speak all key off `Translation` while `renderResult` shows the extras; (b) the toggle→relayout→live-relaunch-or-clearResult idiom is uniform across every mode/picker; (c) learn already demonstrates overriding the selected engine with `p.LearnEngine` and forcing non-streaming for structured JSON (`update.go:452-462`, `engine.go:46-52`, `llm.go:369/444`).
```

16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]

26		TTS       TTS        `toml:"tts"`
27		History   History    `toml:"history"`

29	
30	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
31	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
32	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
33	type Overlay struct {
34		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
35		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
36		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
37		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`

43		Learn         *bool   `toml:"learn,omitempty"`
44		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
45		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
46		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`

239	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
240	func Path() string {
241		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
242			return p
243		}
244		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
245	}
246	
247	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
248	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
249	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
250		p := Path()
251		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
252		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
253			cfg = Default()
254			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
255				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
256			}
257			return cfg, true, nil
258		}
259		if err != nil {
260			return nil, false, err
261		}
262		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
263		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
264			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
265		}
266		return cfg, false, nil
267	}
268	
269	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
270	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
271		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
272			return err
273		}
274		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
275		if err != nil {
276			return err
277		}
278		p := Path()
279		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
280		if err != nil {
281			return err
282		}
283		tmpName := tmp.Name()
284		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
285		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
286			tmp.Close()
287			return err
288		}
289		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
290			return err
291		}
292		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
293	}
294	
295	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
296	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
297		for i := range c.Providers {
298			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
299				return &c.Providers[i]
300			}
301		}
302		return nil
303	}
304	
305	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
306	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
307	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
308		switch tier {
309		case "fast":
310			if p.ModelFast != "" {
311				return p.ModelFast
312			}
313		case "max":
314			if p.ModelMax != "" {
315				return p.ModelMax
316			}
317		}
318		return p.Model
319	}
320
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/config/resolve.go`

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Learn        bool // --learn forces learning mode on (implies pair)
21		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
22	}
23	
24	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
25	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
26	type Mode int
27	
28	const (
29		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
30		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
31	)
32	
33	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
34	type Resolved struct {
35		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
36		Target       string
37		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
38		Provider     *Provider
39		Model        string
40		Tier         string
41		Preset       string
42		Instructions string
43		Pair         bool
44		PairWith     string
45		Learn        bool
46		Stream       bool
47		Color        string
48		Debug        bool
49	
50		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
51		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
52		LiveTranslate bool
53		DebounceMs    int
54	
55		Cfg *Config
56	}
57	
58	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
59	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
60	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
61	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
62	
63	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
64	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
65		switch mode {
66		case ModeCLI:
67			return c.CLI
68		case ModeTUI:
69			return c.TUI
70		}
71		return nil
72	}
73	
74	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
75	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
76	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
77		if ov == nil {
78			return
79		}
80		if ov.Engine != nil {
81			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
82		}
83		if ov.Tier != nil {
84			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
85		}
86		if ov.Preset != nil {
87			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
88		}
89		if ov.Instructions != nil {
90			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
91		}
92		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
93			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
94		}
95		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
96			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
97		}
98		if ov.Pair != nil {
99			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
100		}
101		if ov.PairWith != nil {
102			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
103		}
104		if ov.Learn != nil {
105			g.Learn = *ov.Learn
106		}
107		if ov.Stream != nil {
108			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
109		}
110		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
111			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
112		}
113		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
114			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
115		}
116		if ov.Debug != nil {
117			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
122	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
123	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
124		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
125		// the on-disk config is untouched).
126		g := c.General
127		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
128		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
129	
130		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
131			if flag != "" {
132				return flag
133			}
134			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
135				return v
136			}
137			return cfgVal
138		}
139	
140		r := Resolved{
141			Cfg:           c,
142			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
143			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
144			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
145			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
146			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
147			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
148			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
149			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
150			Learn:         o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "",
151			Color:         g.Color,
152			Stream:        g.Stream,
153			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
154			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
155			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
156		}
157	
158		// Learning mode is inherently bidirectional; it reuses pair's home/away
159		// direction routing, so enabling learn enables pair.
160		if r.Learn {
161			r.Pair = true
162		}
163	
164		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
165		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
166		if provName == "" {
167			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
168				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
169			} else {
170				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
171			}
172		}
173		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
174	
175		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
176		// provider's tier model.
177		cfgModel := ""
178		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
179			cfgModel = *ov.Model
180		}
181		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
182		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
183			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
184		}
185		return r
186	}
187	
188	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
189	// else the first configured provider, else "".
190	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
191		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
192			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
193				return name
194			}
195		}
196		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
197			return c.Providers[0].Name
198		}
199		return ""
200	}
201

```text
1	// Package lang resolves language names/codes with typo tolerance and provides
2	// offline language detection. It is shared by the CLI and the TUI.
3	package lang
4	
5	import (
6		"sort"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
10	)
11	
12	// Lang is one known language: its ISO-639-1 code, English name, and aliases
13	// (native names, ISO-639-2 codes, common misspellings).
14	type Lang struct {
15		Code    string
16		Name    string
17		Aliases []string
18	}
19	
20	// Match is the result of resolving a query to a language.
21	type Match struct {
22		Code  string
23		Name  string
24		Score float64 // 1.0 == exact; lower for fuzzy
25		Exact bool
26	}
27	
28	// table is a pragmatic set of common languages. Fuzzy matching handles typos
29	// on the Latin names, so aliases mainly carry native scripts and ISO-639-2.
30	var table = []Lang{
31		{"en", "english", []string{"eng"}},
32		{"zh", "chinese", []string{"mandarin", "zho", "chi", "中文", "汉语", "漢語", "普通话"}},
33		{"zh-TW", "chinese (traditional)", []string{"traditional chinese", "chinese traditional", "zh-hant", "zht", "繁體中文", "繁体中文", "繁中", "taiwanese mandarin", "taiwan"}},
34		{"zh-CN", "chinese (simplified)", []string{"simplified chinese", "chinese simplified", "zh-hans", "zhs", "简体中文", "简中", "mainland chinese"}},
35		{"es", "spanish", []string{"spa", "español", "castellano"}},
36		{"fr", "french", []string{"fra", "fre", "français"}},
37		{"de", "german", []string{"deu", "ger", "deutsch"}},
38		{"ja", "japanese", []string{"jpn", "日本語"}},
39		{"ko", "korean", []string{"kor", "한국어", "조선말"}},
40		{"pt", "portuguese", []string{"por", "português"}},
41		{"it", "italian", []string{"ita", "italiano"}},
42		{"ru", "russian", []string{"rus", "русский"}},
43		{"ar", "arabic", []string{"ara", "العربية"}},
44		{"hi", "hindi", []string{"hin", "हिन्दी"}},
45		{"nl", "dutch", []string{"nld", "dut", "nederlands"}},
46		{"pl", "polish", []string{"pol", "polski"}},
47		{"tr", "turkish", []string{"tur", "türkçe"}},
48		{"vi", "vietnamese", []string{"vie", "tiếng việt"}},
49		{"th", "thai", []string{"tha", "ไทย"}},
50		{"id", "indonesian", []string{"ind", "bahasa indonesia"}},
51		{"uk", "ukrainian", []string{"ukr", "українська"}},
52		{"sv", "swedish", []string{"swe", "svenska"}},
53		{"cs", "czech", []string{"ces", "cze", "čeština"}},
54		{"el", "greek", []string{"ell", "gre", "ελληνικά"}},
55		{"he", "hebrew", []string{"heb", "עברית", "iw"}},
56		{"ro", "romanian", []string{"ron", "rum", "română"}},
57		{"hu", "hungarian", []string{"hun", "magyar"}},
58		{"fi", "finnish", []string{"fin", "suomi"}},
59		{"da", "danish", []string{"dan", "dansk"}},
60		{"no", "norwegian", []string{"nor", "norsk", "nb", "nn"}},
61		{"fa", "persian", []string{"fas", "per", "farsi", "فارسی"}},
62		{"bn", "bengali", []string{"ben", "বাংলা"}},
63		{"ta", "tamil", []string{"tam", "தமிழ்"}},
64		{"ms", "malay", []string{"msa", "may", "bahasa melayu"}},
65		{"ca", "catalan", []string{"cat", "català"}},
66	}
67	
68	// candidate is one searchable token (code, name, or alias) pointing at a Lang.
69	type candidate struct {
70		token string
71		lang  *Lang
72		// weight scales the fuzzy score: exact code/name matches are preferred.
73		primary bool
74	}
75	
76	var candidates = buildCandidates()
77	
78	func buildCandidates() []candidate {
79		var cs []candidate
80		for i := range table {
81			l := &table[i]
82			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Code), lang: l, primary: true})
83			cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(l.Name), lang: l, primary: true})
84			for _, a := range l.Aliases {
85				cs = append(cs, candidate{token: strings.ToLower(a), lang: l})
86			}
87		}
88		return cs
89	}
90	
91	// Resolve maps a free-form query to a language, tolerating typos. It returns the
92	// best match plus ranked alternatives (for TUI disambiguation). "auto" and ""
93	// pass through as an auto-detect sentinel (Code "auto", Exact true).
94	func Resolve(q string) (Match, []Match) {
95		norm := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(q))
96		if norm == "" || norm == "auto" {
97			return Match{Code: "auto", Name: "auto-detect", Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
98		}
99	
100		// Exact match on any code / name / alias.
101		for _, c := range candidates {
102			if c.token == norm {
103				return Match{Code: c.lang.Code, Name: c.lang.Name, Score: 1, Exact: true}, nil
104			}
105		}
106	
107		// Fuzzy: rank candidates by normalized Levenshtein similarity.
108		type scored struct {
109			lang  *Lang
110			score float64
111		}
112		best := map[string]scored{} // dedupe by language code, keep best score
113		for _, c := range candidates {
114			d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(norm, c.token)
115			maxLen := max(len(norm), len(c.token))
116			if maxLen == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			sim := 1 - float64(d)/float64(maxLen)
120			if !c.primary {
121				sim -= 0.05 // slight preference for code/name over alias
122			}
123			if cur, ok := best[c.lang.Code]; !ok || sim > cur.score {
124				best[c.lang.Code] = scored{lang: c.lang, score: sim}
125			}
126		}
127	
128		ranked := make([]Match, 0, len(best))
129		for _, s := range best {
130			ranked = append(ranked, Match{Code: s.lang.Code, Name: s.lang.Name, Score: s.score})
131		}
132		sort.Slice(ranked, func(i, j int) bool { return ranked[i].Score > ranked[j].Score })
133	
134		if len(ranked) == 0 || ranked[0].Score < 0.5 {
135			// No confident match; echo the query back as an opaque code so callers
136			// can still attempt it (e.g. a code we don't have in the table).
137			return Match{Code: norm, Name: norm, Score: 0}, ranked
138		}
139		top := 3
140		if len(ranked) < top {
141			top = len(ranked)
142		}
143		return ranked[0], ranked[1:top]
144	}
145	
146	// List returns all known languages (for the target-language picker).
147	func List() []Lang {
148		out := make([]Lang, len(table))
149		copy(out, table)
150		return out
151	}
152	
153	// Name returns the English name for a code, or the code itself if unknown.
154	func Name(code string) string {
155		if strings.EqualFold(code, "auto") || code == "" {
156			return "auto-detect"
157		}
158		for i := range table {
159			if strings.EqualFold(table[i].Code, code) {
160				return table[i].Name
161			}
162		}
163		return code
164	}
165
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./internal/engine/prompt.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113	
114	// learnDirection reports the learn-mode direction, decided OFFLINE via the same
115	// script-aware router as pair mode (lang.PairTarget): native (home) input → "teach"
116	// (translate + glosses); foreign (away) input → "correct" (grammar-correct + explain).
117	// Trusting this over the model's own guess keeps the two output shapes deterministic.
118	func learnDirection(req Request) string {
119		if lang.PairTarget(req.PairHome, req.PairAway, req.Text) == req.PairAway {
120			return "teach"
121		}
122		return "correct"
123	}
124	
125	// learnTeachPrompt drives native→foreign tutoring. %[1]s = native (home) language,
126	// %[2]s = foreign (away) language being learned.
127	const learnTeachPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a native %[1]s speaker learn %[2]s.
128	The user writes in %[1]s. Translate their text into natural, idiomatic %[2]s, then teach it.
129	
130	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
131	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
132	{
133	  "direction": "teach",
134	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
135	  "translation": "<the idiomatic %[2]s translation>",
136	  "vocab": [
137	    {"term": "<key %[2]s word or phrase>", "pos": "<part of speech>", "phonetic": "<KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese>", "meaning": "<concise meaning in %[1]s>"}
138	  ],
139	  "examples": [
140	    {"foreign": "<a short natural %[2]s sentence using the translation>", "native": "<its %[1]s translation>"}
141	  ],
142	  "notes": "<one short usage or register tip in %[1]s, optional>"
143	}
144	
145	Rules:
146	- Write every meaning, note, and explanation in %[1]s (the learner's language).
147	- vocab: only the key content words/phrases worth learning (at most 8); skip trivial function words.
148	- examples: 1-2, short and idiomatic.
149	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
150	
151	// learnCorrectPrompt drives foreign→native correction. %[1]s = foreign (away)
152	// language being learned, %[2]s = native (home) language.
153	const learnCorrectPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a %[2]s speaker who is learning %[1]s.
154	The user writes a sentence in %[1]s that may contain mistakes. Correct it and explain, kindly.
155	
156	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
157	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
158	{
159	  "direction": "correct",
160	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
161	  "corrected": "<the grammatically correct, idiomatic %[1]s sentence>",
162	  "translation": "<a %[2]s translation of the intended meaning>",
163	  "issues": [
164	    {"span": "<the problematic fragment of the input>", "fix": "<the corrected fragment>", "explanation": "<why, in %[2]s>"}
165	  ],
166	  "notes": "<one short encouraging tip in %[2]s, optional>"
167	}
168	
169	Rules:
170	- Write every explanation and note in %[2]s (the learner's native language).
171	- If the sentence is already correct, echo it in "corrected" and return "issues": [].
172	- List each distinct mistake as one issue; keep explanations concise and beginner-friendly.
173	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
174	
175	// learnUserPrompt frames the concrete learn request. The system prompt already
176	// names both languages, so the user turn carries only the text.
177	const learnUserPrompt = `Text:
178	%s`
179	
180	// buildLearnPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a learn-mode request,
181	// choosing the teach or correct prompt from the offline-detected direction. Codes
182	// are expanded to readable names so regional variants (zh-TW) read naturally.
183	func buildLearnPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
184		home := lang.Name(req.PairHome) // native
185		away := lang.Name(req.PairAway) // foreign
186		if learnDirection(req) == "teach" {
187			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnTeachPrompt, home, away)
188		} else {
189			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnCorrectPrompt, away, home)
190		}
191		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
192			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
193		}
194		return system, fmt.Sprintf(learnUserPrompt, req.Text)
195	}
196

1	package tui
2	
3	import "charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
4	
5	// keyMap holds the TUI key bindings. It grows across slices; slice 4 uses the
6	// core three, later slices add live-toggle, engine cycle, language swap, etc.
7	type keyMap struct {
8		Translate   key.Binding
9		Newline     key.Binding
10		ToggleLive  key.Binding
11		CycleEngine key.Binding
12		PickLang    key.Binding
13		PickModel   key.Binding
14		PickPreset  key.Binding
15		TogglePair  key.Binding
16		ToggleLearn key.Binding
17		Copy        key.Binding
18		Speak       key.Binding
19		History     key.Binding
20		Clear       key.Binding
21		SwitchFocus key.Binding
22		Quit        key.Binding
23	}
24	
25	func defaultKeys() keyMap {
26		return keyMap{
27			Translate:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("enter", "ctrl+enter"), key.WithHelp("↵", "translate")),
28			Newline:     key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"), key.WithHelp("⌥↵", "newline")),
29			ToggleLive:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+l"), key.WithHelp("^l", "live")),
30			CycleEngine: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+e"), key.WithHelp("^e", "engine")),
31			PickLang:    key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+t"), key.WithHelp("^t", "lang")),
32			PickModel:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+p"), key.WithHelp("^p", "model")),
33			PickPreset:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+o"), key.WithHelp("^o", "style")),
34			TogglePair:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+g"), key.WithHelp("^g", "pair")),
35			ToggleLearn: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+n"), key.WithHelp("^n", "learn")),
36			Copy:        key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+y"), key.WithHelp("^y", "copy")),
37			// ctrl+s is XOFF flow-control only in cooked-mode terminals; Bubble Tea puts
38			// the TTY in raw mode (IXON disabled), so it arrives as a normal key here.
39			Speak:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+s"), key.WithHelp("^s", "speak")),
40			History:     key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+r"), key.WithHelp("^r", "history")),
41			Clear:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+u"), key.WithHelp("^u", "clear")),
42			SwitchFocus: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("tab", "shift+tab"), key.WithHelp("⇥", "focus")),
43			Quit:        key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+c", "esc"), key.WithHelp("^c", "quit")),
44		}
45	}

```text
1	// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
2	// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
3	package tui
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"time"
8	
9		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
10		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/list"
11		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
12		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
13		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
14		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tts"
19	)
20	
21	// NamedEngine is one selectable engine in the TUI's ^e cycle.
22	type NamedEngine struct {
23		Name   string // "auto", "google", "dictionary", …
24		Engine engine.Engine
25		Mode   engine.Mode
26	}
27	
28	// Params configures a TUI session.
29	type Params struct {
30		Engines       []NamedEngine      // selectable engines; index 0 is the default
31		ModelSource   engine.ModelLister // fetches the model list for the model picker (may be nil)
32		ModelProvider string             // provider name the model override applies to
33		Store         store.Store        // nil when history is disabled
34		Source        string
35		Target        string
36		Pair          bool          // bidirectional pair mode
37		PairWith      string        // the "away" language (resolved code)
38		Learn         bool          // start with learning mode (^n) on
39		LearnEngine   engine.Engine // bare LLM engine for learn requests (nil => learn disabled)
40		Model         string        // display model id for the footer (initial)
41		Preset        string        // LLM prompt style
42		Instructions  string        // extra system-prompt guidance
43		Live          bool          // live-debounce default state
44		DebounceMs    int
45		Speaker       tts.Speaker // TTS backend for ^s (nil when disabled)
46		Foreign       string      // preferred "副"/foreign language to speak ("" => derive)
47	}
48	
49	type status int
50	
51	const (
52		statusIdle status = iota
53		statusTyping
54		statusTranslating
55		statusDone
56		statusError
57	)
58	
59	// focusPane selects which pane keyboard navigation drives. Global bindings
60	// (Enter, ^y, ^e, …) work regardless; only ordinary/scroll keys are routed.
61	type focusPane int
62	
63	const (
64		focusInput  focusPane = iota // typing + cursor navigation in the textarea (default)
65		focusOutput                  // keyboard scrolling of the result viewport
66	)
67	
68	// inputH is the fixed height (rows) of the input textarea box; shared by the
69	// layout and the click-to-focus hit test.
70	const inputH = 4
71	
72	// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
73	// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
74	type Model struct {
75		p    Params
76		base context.Context
77	
78		ta          textarea.Model
79		vp          viewport.Model
80		hist        list.Model
81		langList    list.Model
82		modelList   list.Model
83		suggestList list.Model
84		presetList  list.Model
85		sp          spinner.Model
86		keys        keyMap
87		st          styles
88	
89		width, height int
90		ready         bool
91		overlay       overlayKind
92		focus         focusPane // input (default) vs output; drives scroll/typing routing
93		resultH       int       // result viewport height (kept fixed to avoid layout jumps)
94	
95		source   string
96		target   string
97		pair     bool
98		pairWith string
99		learn    bool // learning mode (^n): structured teach/correct via p.LearnEngine
100		preset   string
101		live     bool
102		engIdx   int // index into p.Engines (the active engine)
103	
104		// model picker state (session-cached)
105		cachedModels  []string
106		modelOverride string
107		modelsLoading bool
108		modelsErr     error
109	
110		// curEngine/curModel reflect the engine that actually served the last
111		// result (may differ from the selected engine when the auto chain falls back).
112		curEngine string
113		curModel  string
114	
115		status status
116		result *engine.TranslateResult
117		err    error
118		flash  string // transient footer notice (e.g. "copied ✓")
119	
120		// speakCancel cancels in-flight TTS playback (^s); a new ^s or Clear/Quit
121		// cancels the previous one so playback never overlaps.
122		speakCancel context.CancelFunc
123	
124		// request lifecycle: one monotonic seq drives debounce-collapse, cancel, and
125		// stale-stream-drop. cancel/stream/streamBuf belong to the in-flight request.
126		// streamBuf is a plain string (NOT strings.Builder): the model is copied by
127		// value on every Update, and copying a used Builder panics.
128		seq         int
129		inflight    int
130		cancel      context.CancelFunc
131		stream      <-chan engine.Chunk
132		streamBuf   string
133		debounce    time.Duration
134		lastInput   string     // input text that produced the in-flight/last request
135		lastDoneKey cacheKey   // key of the last COMPLETED result (skip identical re-runs)
136		pendingKey  cacheKey   // cache key of the in-flight request
137		cache       cacheStore // session result cache (live cache-hit = instant, no API call)
138	
139		// Truncation auto-retry: a truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is
140		// re-fired once automatically, since a fresh request almost always completes.
141		// autoRetryKey is the pendingKey we've already retried once (so we retry at
142		// most once per input); retrying drives the "retrying" placeholder label.
143		autoRetryKey cacheKey
144		retrying     bool
145	}
146	
147	// active returns the currently selected engine.
148	func (m Model) active() NamedEngine {
149		if len(m.p.Engines) == 0 {
150			return NamedEngine{}
151		}
152		return m.p.Engines[m.engIdx%len(m.p.Engines)]
153	}
154	
155	// New builds a TUI model.
156	func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
157		ta := textarea.New()
158		ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
159		ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
160		ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
161		// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
162		ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
163		ta.Focus()
164	
165		debounce := time.Duration(p.DebounceMs) * time.Millisecond
166		if debounce <= 0 {
167			debounce = 400 * time.Millisecond
168		}
169	
170		hist := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
171		hist.Title = "History (↵ recall · esc close)"
172		hist.SetShowHelp(false)
173	
174		langList := list.New(langItems(), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
175		langList.Title = "Target language (↵ select · esc close)"
176		langList.SetShowHelp(false)
177	
178		modelList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
179		modelList.Title = "Model (↵ select · esc close)"
180		modelList.SetShowHelp(false)
181	
182		suggestList := list.New(nil, list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
183		suggestList.Title = "Did you mean? (↵ look up · esc close)"
184		suggestList.SetShowHelp(false)
185	
186		preset := p.Preset
187		if preset == "" {
188			preset = engine.PresetConcise
189		}
190		presetList := list.New(presetItems(preset), list.NewDefaultDelegate(), 0, 0)
191		presetList.Title = "Style (↵ select · esc close)"
192		presetList.SetShowHelp(false)
193	
194		sp := spinner.New()
195		sp.Spinner = spinner.MiniDot
196	
197		// Soft-wrap long paragraph lines to the viewport width; without this a single
198		// long (unwrapped) translation line is clipped and its tail is lost on screen.
199		vp := viewport.New()
200		vp.SoftWrap = true
201	
202		return Model{
203			p:           p,
204			base:        ctx,
205			ta:          ta,
206			vp:          vp,
207			hist:        hist,
208			langList:    langList,
209			modelList:   modelList,
210			suggestList: suggestList,
211			presetList:  presetList,
212			sp:          sp,
213			keys:        defaultKeys(),
214			st:          newStyles(),
215			source:      p.Source,
216			target:      p.Target,
217			pair:        p.Pair,
218			pairWith:    p.PairWith,
219			learn:       p.Learn && p.LearnEngine != nil,
220			preset:      preset,
221			live:        p.Live,
222			status:      statusIdle,
223			debounce:    debounce,
224			cache:       cacheStore{},
225		}
226	}
227	
228	// Pair returns the current source and target language codes (read by the caller
229	// after the program exits to persist the last pair).
230	func (m Model) Pair() (source, target string) { return m.source, m.target }
231	
232	// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink + spinner.
233	func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
234		return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, m.sp.Tick, tea.RequestWindowSize)
235	}
236
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/update.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/atotto/clipboard"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tts"
17	)
18	
19	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
20	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
21	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
22		switch msg := msg.(type) {
23		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
24			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
25			m.relayout()
26			m.ready = true
27			return m, nil
28	
29		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
30			return m.handleKey(msg)
31	
32		case debounceMsg:
33			if msg.seq != m.seq {
34				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
35			}
36			return m.launch(false)
37	
38		case historyLoadedMsg:
39			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
40			return m, nil
41	
42		case modelsLoadedMsg:
43			m.modelsLoading = false
44			m.modelsErr = msg.err
45			m.cachedModels = msg.models
46			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
47			return m, nil
48	
49		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
50			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
51	
52		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
53			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
54	
55		case spinner.TickMsg:
56			var cmd tea.Cmd
57			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
58			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
59			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
60			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
61				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
62			}
63			return m, cmd
64	
65		case flashClearMsg:
66			m.flash = ""
67			return m, nil
68	
69		case speakDoneMsg:
70			m.stopSpeak() // release the cancel; playback has ended
71			if msg.err != nil && m.base.Err() == nil {
72				m.flash = "speak failed"
73				return m, flashCmd()
74			}
75			m.flash = ""
76			return m, nil
77	
78		case streamMsg:
79			return m.handleStream(msg)
80		}
81	
82		var cmd tea.Cmd
83		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
84		return m, cmd
85	}
86	
87	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
88	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
89	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
90	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
91		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
92			return m, nil
93		}
94		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
95		case engine.ChunkToken:
96			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
97			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
98			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
99			m.vp.GotoBottom()
100			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
101	
102		case engine.ChunkDone:
103			m.inflight = 0
104			m.status = statusDone
105			m.err = nil
106			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
107			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
108				r := *msg.chunk.Result
109				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
110				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
111				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
112				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
113					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
114					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
115					mm := nm.(Model)
116					mm.retrying = true
117					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
118					return mm, cmd
119				}
120				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
121				m.retrying = false
122				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
123				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
124				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
125				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
126				if !r.Truncated {
127					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
128				}
129				if r.Engine != "" {
130					m.curEngine = r.Engine
131				}
132				if r.Model != "" {
133					m.curModel = r.Model
134				}
135				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
136				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
137				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
138				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
139				// (empty translation, no entry).
140				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
141					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
142				}
143				return m, nil
144			}
145			return m, nil
146	
147		case engine.ChunkError:
148			m.inflight = 0
149			m.retrying = false
150			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
151			m.status = statusError
152			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
153			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
154			return m, nil
155		}
156		return m, nil
157	}
158	
159	// isTextInput reports whether a key press produced a printable, non-whitespace
160	// character (Key.Text is set only for printable characters). Space/tab are
161	// treated as navigation so they can page the viewport instead of typing.
162	func isTextInput(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) bool {
163		return msg.Text != "" && strings.TrimSpace(msg.Text) != ""
164	}
165	
166	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
167		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
168		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
169			switch msg.String() {
170			case "ctrl+c":
171				m.cancelInflight()
172				return m, tea.Quit
173			case "esc":
174				m.overlay = overlayNone
175				return m, nil
176			case "enter":
177				return m.overlaySelect()
178			}
179			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
180			return m, cmd
181		}
182	
183		switch {
184		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
185			m.cancelInflight()
186			m.stopSpeak()
187			return m, tea.Quit
188	
189		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
190			m.overlay = overlayHistory
191			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
192	
193		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
194			m.overlay = overlayLang
195			return m, nil
196	
197		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
198			if !m.learn && m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
199				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode (learn always uses an LLM)
200			}
201			return m.openModelPicker()
202	
203		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
204			if m.learn || m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
205				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles; learn has its own prompt
206			}
207			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
208			m.overlay = overlayPreset
209			return m, nil
210	
211		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLearn):
212			if m.p.LearnEngine == nil {
213				m.flash = "learn needs an LLM provider"
214				return m, flashCmd()
215			}
216			m.learn = !m.learn
217			if m.learn {
218				// Learn is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language.
219				m.pair = true
220				if m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target) {
221					if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
222						m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
223					} else {
224						m.pairWith = "en"
225					}
226				}
227			}
228			m.relayout() // footer content changed
229			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
230				return m.launch(true)
231			}
232			m.clearResult()
233			return m, nil
234	
235		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
236			m.pair = !m.pair
237			// Pair mode is a no-op when "away" equals the target; pick a distinct away.
238			if m.pair && (m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target)) {
239				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
240					m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
241				} else {
242					m.pairWith = "en"
243				}
244			}
245			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
246			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
247				return m.launch(false)
248			}
249			m.clearResult()
250			return m, nil
251	
252		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
253			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
254				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
255					m.flash = "copy failed"
256				} else {
257					m.flash = "copied ✓"
258				}
259				return m, flashCmd()
260			}
261			return m, nil
262	
263		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Speak):
264			return m.speak()
265	
266		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
267			m.live = !m.live
268			return m, nil
269	
270		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
271			if m.learn {
272				m.flash = "learn mode (^n to exit)"
273				return m, flashCmd()
274			}
275			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
276				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
277				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
278				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
279				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
280				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
281				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
282					return m.launch(true)
283				}
284				m.clearResult()
285			}
286			return m, nil
287	
288		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
289			m.cancelInflight()
290			m.stopSpeak()
291			m.seq++
292			m.ta.Reset()
293			m.focus = focusInput
294			m.ta.Focus()
295			m.result = nil
296			m.err = nil
297			m.streamBuf = ""
298			m.status = statusIdle
299			m.vp.SetContent("")
300			return m, nil
301	
302		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwitchFocus):
303			// Toggle which pane the keyboard drives. Blurring the textarea also dims its
304			// border (View keys off ta.Focused()); the result box highlights via m.focus.
305			if m.focus == focusInput {
306				m.focus = focusOutput
307				m.ta.Blur()
308				return m, nil
309			}
310			m.focus = focusInput
311			return m, m.ta.Focus()
312	
313		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
314			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
315			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
316			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
317				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
318				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
319				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
320				return m, nil
321			}
322			return m.launch(true)
323		}
324	
325		// Output pane focused: the keyboard scrolls the result viewport. A printable
326		// character (not whitespace) snaps focus back to the input and is typed there,
327		// so the user can just start typing without pressing Tab first.
328		if m.focus == focusOutput {
329			if isTextInput(msg) {
330				m.focus = focusInput
331				cmd := m.ta.Focus()
332				var tcmd tea.Cmd
333				m.ta, tcmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
334				return m, tea.Batch(cmd, tcmd)
335			}
336			var cmd tea.Cmd
337			m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
338			return m, cmd
339		}
340	
341		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
342		var cmd tea.Cmd
343		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
344	
345		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
346			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
347			m.cancelInflight()
348			m.seq++
349			m.result = nil
350			m.streamBuf = ""
351			m.status = statusIdle
352			m.vp.SetContent("")
353			return m, cmd
354		}
355	
356		if m.live {
357			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
358			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
359			m.status = statusTyping
360			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
361		}
362		return m, cmd
363	}
364	
365	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
366	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
367	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
368	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
369		label := "translating…"
370		if m.retrying {
371			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
372		}
373		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
374	}
375	
376	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
377	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
378	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
379	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
380	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
381		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
382		if text == "" {
383			m.status = statusIdle
384			return m, nil
385		}
386		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
387		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
388			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
389		}
390		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
391		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
392		if !force {
393			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
394				m.cancelInflight()
395				m.status = statusDone
396				m.err = nil
397				m.result = cached
398				if cached.Engine != "" {
399					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
400				}
401				if cached.Model != "" {
402					m.curModel = cached.Model
403				}
404				m.streamBuf = ""
405				m.lastInput = text
406				m.lastDoneKey = key
407				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
408				return m, nil
409			}
410		}
411		m.seq++
412		if m.cancel != nil {
413			m.cancel()
414		}
415		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
416		m.cancel = cancel
417		m.inflight = m.seq
418		m.status = statusTranslating
419		m.streamBuf = ""
420		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
421		m.err = nil
422		m.result = nil
423		m.lastInput = text
424		m.pendingKey = key
425		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
426	
427		seq := m.seq
428		ne := m.active()
429		eng := ne.Engine
430		target := m.target
431		pairOn := m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate
432		if pairOn {
433			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
434			debug.Logf("tui pair route: home=%s away=%s → target=%s", m.target, m.pairWith, target)
435		}
436		req := engine.Request{
437			Text:          text,
438			Source:        m.source,
439			Target:        target,
440			Mode:          ne.Mode,
441			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
442			Model:         m.modelOverride,
443			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
444			Preset:        m.preset,
445			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
446			Pair:          pairOn,
447			PairHome:      m.target,
448			PairAway:      m.pairWith,
449		}
450		// Learn mode overrides the selected engine with a bare LLM engine and asks for a
451		// structured, non-streaming tutor reply routed by pair direction.
452		if m.learn && m.p.LearnEngine != nil {
453			eng = m.p.LearnEngine
454			req.Learn = true
455			req.Mode = engine.ModeTranslate
456			req.Stream = false
457			req.Preset = ""
458			req.Pair = true
459			req.PairHome = m.target
460			req.PairAway = m.pairWith
461			req.Target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // for display/history; engine sets its own
462		}
463		debug.Logf("tui launch: engine=%s mode=%d target=%s preset=%s learn=%v", eng.Name(), req.Mode, req.Target, m.preset, m.learn)
464		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
465		if err != nil {
466			e := err
467			return m, func() tea.Msg {
468				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
469			}
470		}
471		m.stream = ch
472		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
473	}
474	
475	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
476	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
477	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
478		if m.cancel != nil {
479			m.cancel()
480			m.cancel = nil
481		}
482		m.inflight = 0
483	}
484	
485	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
486	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
487	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
488	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
489		m.cancelInflight()
490		m.result = nil
491		m.streamBuf = ""
492		m.err = nil
493		m.status = statusIdle
494		m.vp.SetContent("")
495	}
496	
497	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
498	func (m Model) copyText() string {
499		if m.result == nil {
500			return ""
501		}
502		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
503	}
504	
505	// speak plays the foreign side of the current lookup/translation. When the output
506	// pane is focused it speaks the result explicitly; otherwise it uses the
507	// foreign-language ("副") preference. Playback runs off the UI thread.
508	func (m Model) speak() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
509		if m.p.Speaker == nil {
510			m.flash = "tts disabled"
511			return m, flashCmd()
512		}
513		forced := tts.SideAuto
514		if m.focus == focusOutput {
515			forced = tts.SideResult // explicit output focus → speak the result pane
516		}
517		resText, resLang := "", m.target
518		if m.result != nil {
519			resText = strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
520			if resText == "" && m.result.Dictionary != nil {
521				resText = m.result.Dictionary.Word
522			}
523			if m.result.Target != "" {
524				resLang = m.result.Target
525			}
526		}
527		ch, ok := tts.Select(tts.SelectInput{
528			SourceText: m.ta.Value(),
529			SourceLang: m.source,
530			ResultText: resText,
531			ResultLang: resLang,
532			Foreign:    m.foreignPref(),
533			Forced:     forced,
534		})
535		if !ok {
536			m.flash = "nothing to speak"
537			return m, flashCmd()
538		}
539		m.stopSpeak() // debounce: cancel any playback still in flight
540		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
541		m.speakCancel = cancel
542		m.flash = "speaking…"
543		return m, tea.Batch(flashCmd(), speakCmd(m.p.Speaker, ctx, ch))
544	}
545	
546	// foreignPref is the preferred foreign/副 language: the configured value, else
547	// the pair-mode away language ("" => Select derives it).
548	func (m Model) foreignPref() string {
549		if m.p.Foreign != "" {
550			return m.p.Foreign
551		}
552		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
553			return m.pairWith
554		}
555		return ""
556	}
557	
558	// stopSpeak cancels any in-flight TTS playback.
559	func (m *Model) stopSpeak() {
560		if m.speakCancel != nil {
561			m.speakCancel()
562			m.speakCancel = nil
563		}
564	}
565	
566	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
567	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
568		if m.modelOverride != "" {
569			return m.modelOverride
570		}
571		return m.curModel
572	}
573	
574	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
575	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
576		m.overlay = overlayModel
577		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
578			m.modelsLoading = true
579			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
580		}
581		return m, nil
582	}
583	
584	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
585	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
586		var cmd tea.Cmd
587		switch m.overlay {
588		case overlayHistory:
589			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
590		case overlayLang:
591			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
592		case overlayModel:
593			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
594		case overlaySuggest:
595			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
596		case overlayPreset:
597			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
598		}
599		return cmd
600	}
601	
602	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
603	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
604		switch m.overlay {
605		case overlayHistory:
606			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
607			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
608				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
609				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
610				m.overlay = overlayNone
611				return m.launch(true)
612			}
613		case overlayLang:
614			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
615				m.target = it.code
616				m.overlay = overlayNone
617				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
618				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
619					return m.launch(true)
620				}
621				m.clearResult()
622				return m, nil
623			}
624		case overlayModel:
625			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
626				m.modelOverride = it.id
627				m.overlay = overlayNone
628				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
629					return m.launch(true)
630				}
631				m.clearResult()
632				return m, nil
633			}
634		case overlaySuggest:
635			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
636			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
637				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
638				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
639				m.overlay = overlayNone
640				return m.launch(true)
641			}
642		case overlayPreset:
643			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
644				m.preset = it.id
645				m.overlay = overlayNone
646				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
647				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
648					return m.launch(false)
649				}
650				m.clearResult()
651				return m, nil
652			}
653		}
654		m.overlay = overlayNone
655		return m, nil
656	}
657	
658	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
659	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
660		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
661			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
662			return m, cmd
663		}
664		var cmd tea.Cmd
665		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
666		return m, cmd
667	}
668	
669	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
670	// normal mode a click focuses the pane it lands in (so the keyboard scrolls it),
671	// except a click on the footer row opens the language picker.
672	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
673		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
674			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
675			return m, cmd
676		}
677		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
678			m.overlay = overlayLang
679			return m, nil
680		}
681		// The input box occupies the first inputH+2 rows (border + inputH + border);
682		// everything below it (down to the footer) is the result box.
683		if msg.Y < inputH+2 {
684			if m.focus != focusInput {
685				m.focus = focusInput
686				return m, m.ta.Focus()
687			}
688			return m, nil
689		}
690		if m.focus != focusOutput {
691			m.focus = focusOutput
692			m.ta.Blur()
693		}
694		return m, nil
695	}
696	
697	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
698	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
699		src := m.source
700		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
701			src = res.DetectedSource
702		}
703		target := m.target
704		if res.Target != "" {
705			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
706		}
707		return store.Record{
708			SourceLang:   src,
709			TargetLang:   target,
710			Engine:       res.Engine,
711			Model:        res.Model,
712			Input:        m.lastInput,
713			Output:       res.Translation,
714			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
715			Notes:        res.Notes,
716		}
717	}
718	
719	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
720	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
721	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
722	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
723	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
724	func (m *Model) relayout() {
725		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
726			return
727		}
728		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
729		compW := m.width - 6
730		if compW < 1 {
731			compW = 1
732		}
733		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
734		if resultH < 3 {
735			resultH = 3
736		}
737		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
738		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
739		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
740		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
741		m.resultH = resultH
742		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
743		if overlayH < 1 {
744			overlayH = 1
745		}
746		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
747		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
748		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
749		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
750		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
751	}
752
```
